subject: Social Studies
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
  • Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain topics clearly by organizing ideas, using facts and examples, adding helpful visuals, and finishing with a strong conclusion.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
  • Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
  • Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
  • Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about specific topics by stating their opinion, explaining reasons with facts, addressing other views, and ending with a clear conclusion.

description:

  • Read grade-level high-frequency sight words and words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
  • Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic and organize ideas, concepts, and information to make important connections and distinctions; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic with well-chosen, relevant, and sufficient facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience's knowledge of the topic.
  • Use varied transitions and sentence structures to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to manage the complexity of the topic and convey a style appropriate to the discipline and context as well as to the expertise of likely readers.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain topics like history or science by organizing ideas, using facts and examples, and including helpful features like headings and pictures.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
  • Introduce precise claim(s), distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and create an organization that establishes clear relationships among the claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
  • Develop claim(s) and counterclaims fairly, supplying data and evidence for each while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both claim(s) and counterclaims in a discipline-appropriate form and in a manner that anticipates the audience's knowledge level and concerns.
  • Use words, phrases, and clauses to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write clear arguments about social studies topics by stating their claim, considering other views, using evidence, and ending with a strong conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Read with accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
  • Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
  • Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
  • Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts accurately and smoothly, understands what they read, and uses clues to fix mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
  • Identify common consonant diagraphs, final-e, and common vowel teams.
  • Decode one- and two-syllable words with common patterns.
  • Read grade-level words with inflectional endings.
  • Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses common letter sounds and patterns to read words, including tricky and longer words.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
  • Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
  • Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken and written words.
  • Orally produce single-syllable words, including consonant blends and digraphs
  • Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
  • Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child understands and plays with sounds in words by hearing, blending, and changing sounds to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

description_plain: Your child understands how print works and can recognize the parts of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child learns the basics about print, letters, and sounds to help with reading.

description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly with adequate volume, appropriate pacing, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly tell a story or report on a topic using facts and details, speaking loud enough and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons and proof a speaker uses to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of a text or information they hear, see, or read in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions on grade-level topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about grade-level topics, share their ideas clearly, and build on what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Listening: Students present appropriately in formal speaking situations, listen critically, and respond intelligently as individuals or in group discussions.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly in formal situations, listen carefully, and respond thoughtfully when talking alone or in groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find information from books or the internet, take notes, organize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use technology and the internet to write, share their work, and work with others, typing at least one page in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research, applying grade-level reading standards for literature and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can find proof in stories or information texts to help them think about and research topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child chooses words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally, using a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events; provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can organize events in a story in order, use words that show the sequence, and end with a fitting conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations; use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions to show events and how characters react, using clear words and details that appeal to the senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing the narrator or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate for fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases that clearly show their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create an organizational structure that includes related ideas grouped to support the writer's purpose and linked in a logical order with a concluding statement or section related to the opinion.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas in a clear order to support their opinion and ends with a concluding statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons supported by facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic and state an opinion on the topic.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and clearly states their opinion about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces about topics or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Group related information in paragraphs and sections, linking ideas within categories of information using words and phrases; provide a concluding statement or section; include formatting when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child groups related information into paragraphs, links ideas with words, ends with a conclusion, and uses formatting to help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic; include illustrations and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, and examples to explain a topic and uses pictures or multimedia when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and introduce the topic clearly.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces the topic they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic clearly and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Writing: Students write for different purposes and audiences. Students write clear and focused text to convey a well-defined perspective and appropriate content.
description_plain: Your child writes for different reasons and audiences, creating clear and focused writing that shows their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend literary fiction on grade level, reading independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands grade-level stories independently and well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being and that are basic to a particular topic.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-level words for everyday talk, school subjects, and specific topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies and tools.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast similar themes, topics, and patterns of events in literature, including texts from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts themes, topics, and event patterns in stories, including those from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child connects a story or play with its visual or oral version, noticing where descriptions and directions match.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in grade-level text, including figurative language.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases, including figurative language, in grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose and refer to the structural elements of each when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child explains the main differences between poems, plays, and stories and talks about their parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast an event or topic told from two different points of view.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts an event or topic told from two different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text.
description_plain: Your child describes a character, setting, or event in detail using specific parts from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant details from text to support what the text says explicitly and make inferences.
description_plain: Your child uses important details from the text to support what it says and to make guesses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a text from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or lesson in a story by looking at details and can tell the story in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Reading Literature: Students read and respond to works of literature—with emphasis on comprehension, vocabulary acquisition, and making connections among ideas and between texts with focus on textual evidence.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and talks about what they mean, learns new words, and connects ideas using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend literary nonfiction and informational text on grade level, reading independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand nonfiction and informational books on their grade level by themselves.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies and tools.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies and tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being and that are basic to a particular topic.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words that are common in conversations, school subjects, and specific topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic to demonstrate understanding of that topic.
description_plain: Your child can put together information from two books about the same topic to show they understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret various presentations of information within a text or digital source and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain how pictures, charts, or other information in a text help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in grade-level text, including figurative language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words are used in a creative or special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text structure to interpret information (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution).
description_plain: Your child uses how a text is organized, like order of events or cause and effect, to understand the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast an event or topic told from two different points of view.
description_plain: Your child can compare two different points of view about the same event or topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happened and why in a story or text using specific details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence by quoting accurately from the text to explain what the text says explicitly and make inferences.
description_plain: Your child can find and quote exact parts of a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in text to support what the text says explicitly and make inferences.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how key details support it, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Reading Informational Text: Students read, understand, and respond to informational text—with an emphasis on comprehension, vocabulary acquisition, and making connections among ideas and between texts with a focus on textual evidence.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands informational texts, learns new words, and connects ideas within and between texts using details from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Read with accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
  • Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
  • Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
  • Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books accurately and smoothly, understands what they read, and uses clues in the text to fix mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
  • Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Foundational Skills: Students gain a working knowledge of concepts of print, alphabetic principle, and other basic conventions.
description_plain: Your child learns the basics about print, letters, and sounds to help with reading.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the difference between allies and adversaries.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between friends (allies) and enemies (adversaries).

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify how countries have varying interests.
description_plain: Your child can identify that different countries have different goals and interests.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain what taxes are and why they are necessary.
description_plain: Your child can explain what taxes are and why people have to pay them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the influence of mass media on society.
description_plain: Your child can describe how mass media, like TV and the internet, affects society.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify individual interest groups and how they impact government.
description_plain: Your child can identify groups of people with shared interests and explain how they influence government decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how courts resolve conflicts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how courts help solve disagreements.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the voting process, including registration, primaries, and general elections.
description_plain: Your child can describe how voting works, including signing up to vote, primary elections, and general elections.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify leadership positions and their primary duties at the local, state, and national levels.
description_plain: Your child can name leaders at local, state, and national levels and explain their main jobs.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how local, state, and national governments provide services.
description_plain: Your child can describe how governments at different levels provide services to people.

subject: Social Studies
description: Define and compare the role and structure of local, state, and national governments.
description_plain: Your child can explain what local, state, and national governments do and how they are organized.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the responsibilities and powers of the three branches of government.
description_plain: Your child can describe the responsibilities and powers of the three branches of government.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain why participation in government and civic life is important.
description_plain: Your child can explain why it is important for people to take part in government and community activities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the importance of political leadership and public service.
description_plain: Your child can describe why political leaders and public service are important.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how citizens resolve conflicts in society and government.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people solve disagreements in society and government.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast rights and responsibilites of citizenship in the community, state, and nation.
description_plain: Your child can compare the rights and responsibilities of citizens in their community, state, and country.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how citizens and leaders use political symbols.
description_plain: Your child can describe how citizens and leaders use political symbols.

subject: Social Studies
description: Summarize individual rights guaranteed by the PA Constitution and the U.S. Constitution.
description_plain: Your child can summarize the individual rights guaranteed by the Pennsylvania and U.S. Constitutions.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Explain the basic principles and ideals within documents and the roles played by the framers as found in significant documents:
  • Declaration of Independence
  • United States Constitution
  • Bill of Rights
  • Pennsylvania Constitution
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas and important roles of the people who wrote key documents like the Declaration of Independence and the U.S. Constitution.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Explain how the principles and ideals shape local, state, and national government.
  • Liberty/Freedom
  • Democracy
  • Justice
  • Equality
description_plain: Your child can explain how ideas like freedom, democracy, justice, and equality shape governments at all levels.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast a direct democracy with a republican form of government.
description_plain: Your child can compare a direct democracy, where people vote on everything, with a republic, where people elect leaders to make decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the effect of the rule of law in protecting property rights, individual rights, and the common good.
description_plain: Your child can explain how laws protect people's property, rights, and the common good.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify local bodies of water and landforms to gain an understanding of their impact on the local community.
description_plain: Your child can name local lakes, rivers, and landforms and understand how they affect the community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how weather affects daily life.
description_plain: Your child can describe how weather changes affect daily life.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify land and water forms.
description_plain: Your child can identify different types of land and water features.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the characteristics of homes and businesses located in the community to gain an understanding of physical features.
description_plain: Your child can talk about different homes and businesses in the community and what they look like.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the location of places in the home, school, and community to gain an understanding of relative location.
description_plain: Your child can describe where things are in the home, school, and neighborhood.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret a simple map of a known environment.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use a simple map of a familiar place.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the effect of people on the physical systems within a community.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people change the natural environment in their community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe and explain the effects of the physical systems on people within regions.

Identify the human characteristics of places and regions using the following criteria:

  • Settlement

description_plain: Your child can identify things about people and places like how many people live there, their culture, towns, jobs, and government activities.

subject: Language Arts
description:

  • Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

  • Develop and maintain a consistent voice.

description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write with an awareness of the stylistic aspects of composition.

  • Use sentences of varying lengths and complexities.
  • Develop and maintain a consistent voice.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly using exact words, special vocabulary, varied sentence lengths, a steady voice, and a formal style.

subject: Language Arts

description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies and tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English when speaking based on Grade 8 level and content.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using correct English grammar and rules for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to add interest, clarify information, and strengthen claims and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make them more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation or task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound, valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas and findings clearly with good reasons and details, while making eye contact and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can understand why information is shown in different ways like pictures or numbers and figure out why it was shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s main points and check if their reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions, on grade-level topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks on grade-level topics, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speaking and Listening: Students present appropriately in formal speaking situations, listen critically, and respond intelligently as individuals or in group discussions.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly in formal situations, listen carefully, and respond thoughtfully when talking alone or in groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes over a long time and sometimes quickly, for different subjects, reasons, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find good information from books and the internet, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, including ones they come up with, and ask more questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the internet to write, share ideas, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make it better for their readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research, applying grade-level reading standards for literature and literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child can find evidence in stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write with an awareness of the stylistic aspects of writing.
  • Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects.
  • Use sentences of varying lengths and complexities.
  • Create tone and voice through precise language.
description_plain: Your child can write using different verb forms, sentence types, and choose words carefully to create a clear tone and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically using a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another and show the relationships among experiences and events; provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can organize events in a story in a natural order using transition words and provide a conclusion that reflects on what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques such as dialogue, description, reflection, and pacing to develop experiences, events, and/or characters; use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, description, and pacing with clear details and sensory words to tell about experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, point of view, and introducing characters or narrator.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate for grade 8.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write with an awareness of the stylistic aspects of composition.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
  • Use sentences of varying lengths and complexities.
  • Create tone and voice through precise language.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style
description_plain: Your child can write clearly using precise words and subject-specific vocabulary, vary sentence length, and keep a formal style and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize the claim(s) with clear reasons and evidence clearly; clarify relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence by using words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion; provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can organize their arguments with clear reasons and evidence, connect ideas smoothly, and end with a strong conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims and support claim with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different opinions, support their own with good reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce and state an opinion on a topic.
description_plain: Your child can introduce and clearly state their opinion on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that clearly support their opinions with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write with an awareness of the stylistic aspects of composition.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
  • Use sentences of varying lengths and complexities.
  • Create tone and voice through precise language.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly using exact words, different sentence types, and keeps a formal tone throughout their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts; provide a concluding statement or section; include formatting when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas into groups, uses transitions to connect them, ends with a clear conclusion, and formats their writing to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Science
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
  • Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write informative science texts that introduce a topic, organize ideas clearly, use facts and examples, include helpful visuals, use clear transitions, and end with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
  • Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
  • Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
  • Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write science arguments by stating their claim, comparing it to other views, supporting it with facts and evidence, using clear language to connect ideas, keeping a formal tone, and ending with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic and organize ideas, concepts, and information to make important connections and distinctions; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic with well-chosen, relevant, and sufficient facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience's knowledge of the topic.
  • Use varied transitions and sentence structures to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to manage the complexity of the topic and convey a style appropriate to the discipline and context as well as to the expertise of likely readers.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts about science or history by introducing topics, organizing ideas, using facts and examples, and finishing with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic and organize ideas, concepts, and information to make important connections and distinctions; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic with well-chosen, relevant, and sufficient facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience's knowledge of the topic.
  • Use varied transitions and sentence structures to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to manage the complexity of the topic and convey a style appropriate to the discipline and context as well as to the expertise of likely readers.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts about science or history by introducing topics, organizing ideas, using facts and examples, and finishing with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
  • Introduce precise claim(s), distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and create an organization that establishes clear relationships among the claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
  • Develop claim(s) and counterclaims fairly, supplying data and evidence for each while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both claim(s) and counterclaims in a discipline-appropriate form and in a manner that anticipates the audience's knowledge level and concerns.
  • Use words, phrases, and clauses to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about science topics by stating clear claims, considering opposing views, using evidence, and ending with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science

  • Use words, phrases, and clauses to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.

  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about science topics by stating clear claims, considering opposing views, using evidence, and ending with a strong conclusion.

  • Read grade-level high-frequency sight words with automaticity.
  • Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.

subject: Language Arts

Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

  • Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.

  • Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sound (phonemes) in the three-phoneme (CVC) words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say rhyming words, count and break words into parts, and say the beginning, middle, and ending sounds in simple words.

  • Recognize and name all uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works by reading left to right and top to bottom, knowing words are made of letters, seeing spaces between words, and naming all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic and organize complex ideas, concepts, and information so that each new element builds on that which precedes it to create a unified whole; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic thoroughly by selecting the most significant and relevant facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience's knowledge of the topic.
  • Use varied transitions and sentence structures to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among complex ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language, domain-specific vocabulary and techniques such as metaphor, simile, and analogy to manage the complexity of the topic; convey a knowledgeable stance in a style that responds to the discipline and context as well as to the expertise of likely readers.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation provided (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain topics like history or science by organizing ideas clearly, using headings and pictures when helpful, giving important facts and examples, linking ideas smoothly, using precise language, and ending with a strong conclusion.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
  • Introduce precise, knowledgeable claim(s), establish the significance of the claim(s), distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and create an organization that logically sequences the claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
  • Develop claim(s) and counterclaims fairly and thoroughly, supplying the most relevant data and evidence for each while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both claim(s) and counterclaims in a discipline-appropriate form that anticipates the audience's knowledge level, concerns, values, and possible biases.
  • Use words, phrases, and clauses as well as varied syntax to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about specific subjects by stating clear claims, explaining why they matter, comparing different views fairly, using strong reasons and evidence, linking ideas clearly, keeping a formal tone, and finishing with a clear conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic and organize complex ideas, concepts, and information so that each new element builds on that which precedes it to create a unified whole; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic thoroughly by selecting the most significant and relevant facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience's knowledge of the topic.
  • Use varied transitions and sentence structures to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among complex ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language, domain-specific vocabulary and techniques such as metaphor, simile, and analogy to manage the complexity of the topic; convey a knowledgeable stance in a style that responds to the discipline and context as well as to the expertise of likely readers.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation provided (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain topics like history or science by organizing ideas clearly, using helpful headings and pictures, choosing important facts, linking ideas smoothly, using precise language, and ending with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
  • Introduce a topic and organize complex ideas, concepts, and information so that each new element builds on that which precedes it to create a unified whole; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
  • Develop the topic thoroughly by selecting the most significant and relevant facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience's knowledge of the topic.
  • Use varied transitions and sentence structures to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among complex ideas and concepts.
  • Use precise language, domain-specific vocabulary and techniques such as metaphor, simile, and analogy to manage the complexity of the topic; convey a knowledgeable stance in a style that responds to the discipline and context as well as to the expertise of likely readers.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation provided (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain topics like history or science by organizing ideas clearly, using helpful headings and pictures, choosing important facts, linking ideas smoothly, using precise language, and ending with a strong conclusion.

subject: Science
description:
Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
  • Introduce precise, knowledgeable claim(s), establish the significance of the claim(s), distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and create an organization that logically sequences the claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
  • Develop claim(s) and counterclaims fairly and thoroughly, supplying the most relevant data and evidence for each while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both claim(s) and counterclaims in a discipline-appropriate form that anticipates the audience's knowledge level, concerns, values, and possible biases.
  • Use words, phrases, and clauses as well as varied syntax to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.
  • Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
  • Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write clear arguments about science topics by stating their claim, explaining why it matters, comparing it to other views, using strong evidence, linking ideas smoothly, keeping a formal tone, and finishing with a solid conclusion.

description_plain: With support from adults and friends, your child can plan, change, and improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research, applying grade-level reading standards for literature and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how to find proof from stories or information texts to help explain their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child shows they know how to use correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that make their writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally, using temporal words and phrases to signal event order; provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can put events in order using words like first, next, and last, and finish their story with a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses conversations and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing who is telling the story and the characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or made-up experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate for third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of words and sentence types to appeal to the audience.
description_plain: Your child uses different kinds of words and sentences to keep the reader interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create an organizational structure that includes reasons linked in a logical order with a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can organize reasons in a clear order and finish with a concluding statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support an opinion with reasons.
description_plain: Your child supports their opinion by giving reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic and state an opinion on the topic.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and clearly state their opinion about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on familiar topics or texts.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces about topics or books they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a grade-appropriate command of the conventions of standard English grammar, usage, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand and use correct grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child chooses words and phrases that make their writing more effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create an organizational structure that includes information grouped and connected logically with a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child organizes information into groups that make sense and ends with a clear conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, details, and illustrations, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, and pictures to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and introduce the topic.
description_plain: Your child can identify and introduce the topic they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts that clearly explain a topic and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Writing: Students write for different purposes and audiences. Students write clear and focused text to convey a well-defined perspective and appropriate content.
description_plain: Your child writes for different reasons and audiences, creating clear and focused writing that shows their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend literary fiction on grade level, reading independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands grade-level stories on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-appropriate words for talking, school subjects, and special topics, including words that show location and time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies and tools.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies and tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child compares the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures in a story help show the mood or important parts of characters or places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in grade-level text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral meaning as well as shades of meaning among related words.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases in stories, including when words are used literally or in a special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of texts when writing or speaking about a text using such terms as chapter, scene, and stanza and describe how each successive part builds upon earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child refers to parts of a story like chapters or scenes and explains how each part connects to the ones before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the point of view of the author.
description_plain: Your child explains the author's point of view or opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child describes characters and explains how what they do moves the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about the text and make inferences from text, referring to text to support responses.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about a story and uses the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central message, lesson, or moral in literary text; explain how it is conveyed in text.
description_plain: Your child finds the main message or lesson in a story and explains how the story shows it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Reading Literature: Students read and respond to works of literature—with emphasis on comprehension, vocabulary acquisition, and making connections among ideas and between texts with focus on textual evidence.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and talks about what they mean, learns new words, and connects ideas using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend literary nonfiction and informational text on grade level, reading independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands nonfiction and informational texts at their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies and tools.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words in nonfiction texts using different strategies and tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-appropriate words for talking, school subjects, and special topics, including words that show location and time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares the main points and important details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how an author connects sentences and paragraphs in a text to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child describes how an author connects sentences and paragraphs to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from text features to demonstrate understanding of a text.
description_plain: Your child uses features like headings and pictures in a text to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in grade-level text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral meaning as well as shades of meaning among related words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in third-grade reading, including when words are used literally or in a different way, and notice small differences between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools to locate and interpret information.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book or search tools to find and understand information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the point of view of the author.
description_plain: Your child understands and can explain the author's point of view in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of events, concepts, or steps in a procedure is connected within a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events or steps in a story or instructions are connected using words about time, order, and cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about the text and make inferences from text; refer to text to support responses.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read and make guesses based on the text, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, tell the important details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Reading Informational Text: Students read, understand, and respond to informational text—with an emphasis on comprehension, vocabulary acquisition, and making connections among ideas and between texts with a focus on textual evidence.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands informational texts, learns new words, and connects ideas within and between texts using details from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Read with accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
  • Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
  • Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
  • Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child reads third-grade level books accurately and smoothly, understands what they read, and uses clues in the text to fix mistakes when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
  • Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
  • Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
  • Decode multisyllable words.
  • Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words, can sound out words with Latin endings, read longer words, and read tricky words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Foundational Skills: Students gain a working knowledge of concepts of print, alphabetic principle, and other basic conventions.
description_plain: Your child learns the basics about print, letters, and sounds to help with reading.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and ideas in different ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns and use them to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the best tools, like rulers or calculators, to help with math.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data using tables/charts.
description_plain: Your child can show and understand information using tables and charts.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest half hour using both analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest half hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Order lengths and measure them both indirectly and by repeating length units.
description_plain: Your child can put lengths in order and measure them by comparing or by using repeated units.

subject: Math
description: Measurement, Data, and Probability
description_plain: Your child learns about measuring, collecting data, and understanding chance.

subject: Math
description: Use the understanding of fractions to partition shapes into halves and quarters.
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes into halves and quarters to understand fractions.

subject: Math
description: Compose and distinguish between two- and three-dimensional shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can put together and tell the difference between flat shapes and solid shapes by looking at their features.

subject: Math
description: Geometry
description_plain: Geometry

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child understands how addition and subtraction work and how they relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction within 20.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Algebraic Concepts
description_plain: Algebraic Concepts

subject: Math
description: Use place-value concepts and properties of operations to add and subtract within 100.
description_plain: Your child can use place value and math rules to add and subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use place-value concepts to represent amounts of tens and ones and to compare two digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use tens and ones to show numbers and compare two-digit numbers.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence to read and write numerals to represent objects.
description_plain: Your child can count higher numbers and write numbers to show how many objects there are.

subject: Math
description: Numbers and Operations
description_plain: Numbers and Operations

description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns and use them to help solve math problems.
