description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the best tools, like rulers or calculators, to help with math.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Extend the concepts of addition and subtraction to problems involving length.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction to solve problems involving length.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data using line plots, picture graphs, and bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can create and understand line plots, picture graphs, and bar graphs to show data.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and make change using coins and paper currency with appropriate symbols.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems and make change using coins and paper money, using the correct symbols.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest five minutes using both analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units using appropriate tools.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate lengths using standard tools and units.

subject: Math
description: Measurement, Data, and Probability
description_plain: Your child learns about measuring, collecting data, and understanding chance.

subject: Math
description: Use the understanding of fractions to partition shapes into halves, quarters, and thirds.
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes into halves, quarters, and thirds to understand fractions.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and draw two- and three-dimensional shapes having specified attributes.
description_plain: Your child can look at and draw flat and solid shapes with certain features.

subject: Math
description: Geometry
description_plain: Geometry

subject: Math
description: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can work with equal groups of objects to get ready for learning multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Use mental strategies to add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can use mental math to add and subtract numbers within 20.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction within 100.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Algebraic Concepts
description_plain: Algebraic Concepts

subject: Math
description: Use place-value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract within 1000.
description_plain: Your child can use place value and math rules to add and subtract numbers up to 1000.

subject: Math
description: Use place-value concepts to read, write, and skip count to 1000.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and skip count numbers up to 1000 using place value.

subject: Math
description: Use place-value concepts to represent amounts of tens and ones and to compare three digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use tens and ones to show numbers and compare three-digit numbers.

subject: Math
description: Numbers and Operations
description_plain: Numbers and Operations

subject: Math
description: Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by putting together two rectangular boxes that don’t overlap.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = B × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems. Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole number sides to solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Use, describe, and develop procedures to solve problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child can use and explain steps to solve problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Display and interpret data shown in tallies, tables, charts, pictographs, bar graphs, and line graphs, and use a title, appropriate scale, and labels. A grid will be provided to display data on bar graphs or line graphs.
description_plain: Your child can show and understand data using tallies, tables, charts, pictographs, bar graphs, and line graphs with titles and labels.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving computation of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can solve fraction problems by using information from line plots.

subject: Math
description: Organize, display, and answer questions based on data.
description_plain: Your child can organize, show, and answer questions about data.

subject: Math
description: Convert between different-sized measurement units within a given measurement system. A table of equivalencies will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system using a conversion table.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems using simple conversions (may include multistep, real-world problems).
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve changing units, including real-life and multi-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their features and how they relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Use basic properties to classify two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can use simple rules to sort flat shapes into groups based on their properties.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world and mathematical problems by plotting points in quadrant I of the coordinate plane and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on the top-right part of a grid to solve real-life and math problems and explain what the points mean.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of the coordinate plane (x-axis, y-axis, and the origin) and the ordered pair (x-coordinate and y-coordinate). Limit the coordinate plane to quadrant I.
description_plain: Your child understands the parts of a grid (x-axis, y-axis, and origin) and can identify the two numbers that show a point's location in the top-right section.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of a coordinate grid and describe or interpret points given an ordered pair.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of a coordinate grid and explain where points are using pairs of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms of two patterns with the same starting numbers that follow different rules.
description_plain: Your child can find connections between two number patterns that start the same but change in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules.
description_plain: Your child can make two number patterns by following two different rules.

subject: Math
description: Create, extend, and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can create, continue, and study number patterns.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that model calculations with numbers and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what they mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple grouping symbols (parentheses, brackets, or braces) in numerical expressions and evaluate expressions containing these symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use different grouping symbols like parentheses and brackets in math expressions and solve them correctly.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and complete calculations by applying the order of operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems by following the correct order of steps in calculations.

subject: Math
description: Divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide fractions that are one whole part by whole numbers and also divide whole numbers by these fractions.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding of multiplication as scaling (resizing).
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying can mean making something bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a fraction (including mixed numbers) by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply one fraction by another, including mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions (including mixed numbers).
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems where dividing whole numbers results in fractions or mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve multiplication and division problems involving fractions and whole numbers (straight computation or word problems).
description_plain: Your child can solve multiplication and division problems with fractions and whole numbers, including word problems.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions (including mixed numbers) with unlike denominators. (May include multiple methods and representations.)
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers even when the bottom numbers (denominators) are different.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction problems involving fractions (straight computation or word problems)
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with fractions, including word problems.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths (no divisors with decimals).
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals up to two decimal places, but won't divide by decimals.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors.
description_plain: Your child can divide whole numbers where the number being divided has up to four digits and the divisor has up to two digits.

subject: Math
description: Multiply multi-digit whole numbers (not to exceed three-digit by three-digit).
description_plain: Your child can multiply whole numbers with up to three digits by other numbers with up to three digits.

subject: Math
description: Use whole numbers and decimals to compute accurately (straight computation or word problems).
description_plain: Your child can accurately solve math problems using whole numbers and decimals, including word problems.

subject: Math
description: Round decimals to any place (limit rounding to ones, tenths, hundredths, or thousandths place).
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to the nearest whole number, tenth, hundredth, or thousandth.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place using >, =, and < symbols.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals up to three decimal places using greater than, less than, or equal signs.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, word form, and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals up to three decimal places in numbers, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10 and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain how multiplying or dividing by 10, 100, or 1000 changes a number and where the decimal point moves.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child understands that each digit in a number is one-tenth the value of the digit to its left.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate understanding of place-value of whole numbers and decimals, and compare quantities or magnitudes of numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands place value for whole numbers and decimals and can compare the size of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Relate the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can choose the best way to describe data based on how the data looks and where it came from.

subject: Math
description: Describe any overall pattern and any deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can describe the main pattern in data and point out anything that is different or unusual, explaining what it means.

subject: Math
description: Determine quantitative measures of center (e.g., median, mean, mode) and variability (e.g., range, interquartile range, mean absolute deviation).
description_plain: Your child can find average values like mean, median, and mode, and measures that show how spread out data is.

subject: Math
description: Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including line plots, histograms, and box-and whisker plots.
description_plain: Your child can show numbers on different types of graphs like line plots, histograms, and box plots.

subject: Math
description: Display, analyze, and summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context.
description_plain: Your child can display, study, and summarize sets of numbers based on what they represent.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate understanding of statistical variability by summarizing and describing distributions
description_plain: Your child understands how data can vary and can summarize and explain data sets.

subject: Math
description: Statistics and Probability
description_plain: Statistics and Probability

subject: Math
description: Determine the surface area of triangular and rectangular prisms (including cubes). Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can find the surface area of 3D shapes like triangular and rectangular boxes, including cubes, using given formulas.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made of rectangles and triangles.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns made of rectangles and triangles that can be folded to make 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Given coordinates for the vertices of a polygon in the plane, use the coordinates to find side lengths and area of the polygon (limited to triangles and special quadrilaterals). Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can use points on a grid to find side lengths and area of shapes like triangles and special four-sided figures using formulas.

subject: Math
description: Determine the volume of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths. Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes with fractional side lengths using formulas.

subject: Math
description: Determine the area of irregular or compound polygons. Example: Find the area of a room in the shape of an irregular polygon by composing and/or decomposing.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of shapes that are irregular by breaking them into simpler shapes or combining shapes.

subject: Math
description: Determine the area of triangles and special quadrilaterals (i.e., square, rectangle, parallelogram, rhombus, and trapezoid). Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of triangles and special four-sided shapes like squares, rectangles, and trapezoids using formulas.

subject: Math
description: Find area, surface area, and volume by applying formulas and using various strategies.
description_plain: Your child can find area, surface area, and volume of shapes by using formulas and different methods.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about area, surface area, and volume using real-life examples and math.

subject: Math
description: Geometry
description_plain: Geometry

subject: Math
description: Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables and/or relate these to an equation.
description_plain: Your child can look at graphs and tables to understand how one quantity changes when another changes, and connect this to an equation.

subject: Math
description: Write an equation to express the relationship between the dependent and independent variables. Example: In a problem involving motion at a constant speed of 65 units, write the equation d = 65t to represent the relationship between distance and time.
description_plain: Your child can write an equation that shows how one quantity depends on another, like distance equals speed times time.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another.
description_plain: Your child can use letters (variables) to show how two amounts change together in a real-life problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can show and study how one quantity depends on another using numbers and graphs.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem and/or represent solutions of such inequalities on number lines.
description_plain: Your child can write and show inequalities like x > 5 to describe limits in real-life or math problems and draw these on number lines.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p, q, and x are all non-negative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by writing and solving simple equations like x + p = q or px = q with positive numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write algebraic expressions to represent real-world or mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can write math expressions using letters to represent real-life or math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child can check if a number makes an equation or inequality true by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Create, solve, and interpret one variable equations or inequalities in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can create, solve, and understand equations or inequalities with one variable in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can understand and solve equations and inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions. Example 1: Apply the distributive property to the expression 3 (2 + x) to produce the equivalent expression 6 + 3x. Example 2: Apply the distributive property to the expression 24x + 18y to produce the equivalent expression 6(4x + 3y). Example 3: Apply properties of operations to y + y + y to produce the equivalent expression 3y.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to rewrite expressions in different but equal ways, like using distributive property.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables, including expressions that arise from formulas used in real-world problems. Example: Evaluate the expression b2 – 5 when b = 4.
description_plain: Your child can find the value of expressions by putting in specific numbers, including those from real-life formulas.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (e.g., sum, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient, quantity). Example: Describe the expression 2(8 + 7) as a product of two factors.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of math expressions using terms like sum, product, factor, and coefficient.

subject: Math
description: Write algebraic expressions from verbal descriptions. Example: Express the description “five less than twice a number” as 2y – 5.
description_plain: Your child can turn words into math expressions, like writing 'five less than twice a number' as 2y – 5.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve math expressions that use whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Identify, write, and evaluate numerical and algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can recognize, write, and solve both number and letter math expressions.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to numerical and algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with number and letter expressions.

subject: Math
description: Expressions and Equations
description_plain: Expressions and Equations

subject: Math
description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity); solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percentage.
description_plain: Your child can find a percent of a number and solve problems where you know part of a whole and the percent.

subject: Math
description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed. Example: If it took 7 hours to mow 4 lawns, then at that rate, how many lawns could be mowed in 35 hours? At what rate were lawns being mowed?
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about rates, like how fast something happens or the price per item.

subject: Math
description: Construct tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole-number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and/or plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.
description_plain: Your child can make tables to compare ratios, find missing numbers, and plot these pairs on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Find the unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b (with b ≠ 0) and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship. Example 1: “This recipe has a ratio of 3 cups of flour to 4 cups of sugar, so there is 3/4 cup of flour for each cup of sugar.” Example 2: “We paid $75 for 15 hamburgers, which is a rate of $5 per hamburger.”
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate in a ratio and explain it, like how much of one thing there is for each of another.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio language and notation (such as 3 to 4, 3:4, 3/4) to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities. Example 1: “The ratio of girls to boys in a math class is 2:3 because for every 2 girls there are 3 boys.” Example 2: “For every five votes candidate A received, candidate B received four votes.”
description_plain: Your child can use ratio words and symbols to describe how two amounts compare, like 3 to 4 or 3:4.

subject: Math
description: Represent and/or solve real world and mathematical problems using rates, ratios, and/or percents.
description_plain: Your child can use rates, ratios, and percents to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems
description_plain: Your child understands ratios and can use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Ratios and Proportional Relationships
description_plain: Your child learns about ratios and proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by plotting points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can plot points in all four parts of a coordinate plane and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line and as a magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world situation. Example: For an account balance of – 30 dollars, write |– 30| = 30 to describe the size of the debt in dollars, and recognize that an account balance less than – 30 dollars represents a debt greater than 30 dollars.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as the distance from zero and can use it to describe sizes of positive or negative amounts in real life.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts. Example: Write – 3°C > – 7°C to express the fact that – 3°C is warmer than – 7°C.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain which rational numbers are bigger or smaller in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how to order rational numbers and what absolute value means.

subject: Math
description: Locate and plot integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line; locate and plot pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and plot whole numbers and fractions on number lines and coordinate planes.

subject: Math
description: Determine the opposite of a number and recognize that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself (e.g., – ( – 3) = 3; 0 is its own opposite).
description_plain: Your child knows how to find the opposite of a number and understands that the opposite of the opposite is the original number.

subject: Math
description: Represent quantities in real-world contexts using positive and negative numbers, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation (e.g., temperature above/below zero, elevation above/below sea level, credits/debits, positive/negative electric charge).
description_plain: Your child can use positive and negative numbers to describe real-life situations and explain what zero means in each case.

subject: Math
description: Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values and locations on the number line and coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child understands that positive and negative numbers show opposite directions or values and can find them on number lines and graphs.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to the system of rational numbers
description_plain: Your child builds on what they know about numbers to understand rational numbers better.

subject: Math
description: Apply the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers, 1 through 100, with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor. Example: Express 36 + 8 as 4(9 + 2).
description_plain: Your child can use the distributive property to rewrite sums of two whole numbers with a common factor as a product.

subject: Math
description: Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor of two numbers up to 100 and the least common multiple of two numbers up to 12.

subject: Math
description: Apply number theory concepts (specifically, factors and multiples).
description_plain: Your child learns about factors and multiples and how to use them.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving operations (+, –, ×, and ÷) with whole numbers, decimals (through thousandths), straight computation, or word problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with whole numbers and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Compute with multi-digit numbers using the four arithmetic operations with or without a calculator.
description_plain: Your child can do math with multi-digit numbers using all four operations, with or without a calculator.

subject: Math
description: Compute with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples
description_plain: Your child can do math with large numbers and find numbers that divide evenly into others or that several numbers share.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions (including mixed numbers), and solve word problems involving division of fractions by fractions. Example 1: Given a story context for (2/3) ÷ (3/4), explain that (2/3) ÷ (3/4) = 8/9 because 3/4 of 8/9 is 2/3. (In general, (a/b) ÷ (c/d) = (a/b) × (d/c) = ad/bc.) Example 2: How wide is a rectangular strip of land with length 3/4 mi and area 1/2 square mi? Example 3: How many 2 1/4-foot pieces can be cut from a 15 1/2-foot board?
description_plain: Your child can divide fractions, including mixed numbers, and solve story problems about dividing fractions by fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving division of fractions.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems that involve dividing fractions.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands how to divide fractions by fractions using what they know about multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: The Number System
description_plain: The Number System

subject: Math
description: Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by putting together two rectangular boxes that don’t overlap.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = B × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems. Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole-number sides in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use, describe, and develop procedures to solve problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child can use and explain steps to solve problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands what volume means and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Display and interpret data shown in tallies, tables, charts, pictographs, bar graphs, and line graphs, and use a title, appropriate scale, and labels. A grid will be provided to display data on bar graphs or line graphs.
description_plain: Your child can show and understand data using tallies, tables, charts, pictures, bar graphs, and line graphs with titles and labels.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving computation of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with fractions by using information from line plots.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Convert between different-sized measurement units within a given measurement system. A table of equivalencies will be provided. Example: Convert 5 cm to meters
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system, like centimeters to meters.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system
description_plain: Your child can convert measurements between units that are alike, such as inches to feet.

subject: Math
description: Data and Measurement
description_plain: Data and Measurement

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties. Example 1: All polygons have at least three sides, and pentagons are polygons, so all pentagons have at least three sides. Example 2: A rectangle is a parallelogram, which is a quadrilateral, which is a polygon; so, a rectangle can be classified as a parallelogram, as a quadrilateral, and as a polygon.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their features and explain why they belong in those groups.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes by their properties.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world and mathematical problems by plotting points in quadrant I of the coordinate plane and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on the top-right part of a graph and explain what the points mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of the coordinate plane (x-axis, y-axis, and the origin) and the ordered pair (x-coordinate and y-coordinate). Limit the coordinate plane to quadrant I.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of a graph like the x-axis, y-axis, and origin, and understand ordered pairs in the first quadrant.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs to solve real-life and math problems by plotting points.

subject: Math
description: Geometry
description_plain: Geometry

subject: Math
description: Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms of two patterns with the same starting numbers that follow different rules. Example: Given two patterns in which the first pattern follows the rule “add 8” and the second pattern follows the rule “add 2,” observe that the terms in the first pattern are 4 times the size of the terms in the second pattern.
description_plain: Your child can notice how two number patterns with the same start but different rules relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Example: Given the rule “add 3” and the starting number 0 and given the rule “add 6” and the starting number 0, generate terms in the resulting sequences
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns using two different rules.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can study and understand patterns and how numbers relate.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that model calculations with numbers and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them. Example 1: Express the calculation “add 8 and 7, then multiply by 2” as 2 × (8 + 7). Example 2: Recognize that 3 × (18,932 + 921) is three times as large as 18,932 + 921 without having to calculate the indicated sum or product.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what they mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple grouping symbols (parentheses, brackets, or braces) in numerical expressions and evaluate expressions containing these symbols
description_plain: Your child can use and solve math expressions that have different grouping symbols like parentheses and brackets.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions with numbers.

subject: Math
description: Operations and Algebraic Thinking
description_plain: Your child learns about math operations and algebra thinking.

subject: Math
description: Divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide unit fractions by whole numbers and divide whole numbers by unit fractions.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding of multiplication as scaling (resizing). Example 1: Comparing the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor without performing the indicated multiplication. Example 2: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as resizing and can explain why multiplying by fractions changes the size of a number.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a fraction (including mixed numbers) by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply fractions, including mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions (including mixed numbers).
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems where dividing whole numbers results in fractions or mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions using what they already know about multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions (including mixed numbers) with unlike denominators. (May include multiple methods and representations.) Example: 2/3 + 5/4 = 8/12 + 15/12 = 23/12
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers even when the bottom numbers are different.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions
description_plain: Your child uses equal fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Numbers and Operations - Fractions
description_plain: Your child learns about fractions and how to work with them.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths (no divisors with decimals).
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals up to two decimal places, but not divide by decimals.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors.
description_plain: Your child can divide whole numbers with up to four digits by numbers with up to two digits.

subject: Math
description: Multiply multi-digit whole numbers (not to exceed three-digit by three-digit).
description_plain: Your child can multiply whole numbers with up to three digits by other whole numbers with up to three digits.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths
description_plain: Your child can do math operations with big whole numbers and decimals up to two decimal places.

subject: Math
description: Round decimals to any place (limit rounding to ones, tenths, hundredths, or thousandths place).
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to the nearest whole number, tenth, hundredth, or thousandth.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place using >, =, and < symbols.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals up to the thousandths place using greater than, less than, or equal signs.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, word form, and expanded form. Example: 347.392 = 300 + 40 + 7 + 0.3 + 0.09 + 0.002 = 3 × 100 + 4 × 10
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals up to thousandths in numbers, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10 and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10. Example 1: 4 × 102 = 400 Example 2: 0.05 ÷ 103 = 0.00005
description_plain: Your child can explain how multiplying or dividing by powers of 10 changes the number and where the decimal point moves.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left. Example: Recognize that in the number 770, the 7 in the tens place is 1/10 the 7 in the hundreds place.
description_plain: Your child can explain that in a multi-digit number, a digit is worth one-tenth of the digit to its left, like the 7 in the tens place is one-tenth the value of the 7 in the hundreds place in 770.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place-value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how each digit in a number has a different place value, like ones, tens, and hundreds.

subject: Math
description: Numbers and Operations in Base Ten
description_plain: Your child works with numbers and understands how our base-ten number system works.

subject: Math
description: Find probabilities of independent compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of combined events happening by using lists, tables, tree diagrams, or simulations.

subject: Math
description: Find the probability of a simple event, including the probability of a simple event not occurring. Example: What is the probability of not rolling a 1 on a number cube?
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of a simple event happening or not happening, like not rolling a 1 on a number cube.

subject: Math
description: Determine the probability of a chance event given relative frequency. Predict the approximate relative frequency given the probability. Example: When rolling a number cube 600 times, predict that a 3 or 6 would be rolled roughly 200 times but probably not exactly 200 times.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the chance of an event based on how often it happens and predict how often it might happen in many tries.

subject: Math
description: Use probability to predict outcomes.
description_plain: Your child uses probability to guess what might happen in chance events.

subject: Math
description: Predict or determine whether some outcomes are certain, more likely, less likely, equally likely, or impossible (i.e., a probability near 0 indicates an unlikely event, a probability around 1/2 indicates an event that is neither unlikely nor likely, and a probability near 1 indicates a likely event).
description_plain: Your child can tell if an outcome is certain, likely, unlikely, equally likely, or impossible.

subject: Math
description: Predict or determine the likelihood of outcomes.
description_plain: Your child can predict how likely different outcomes are.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models.
description_plain: Your child explores chance events and learns how to create and use probability models.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numerical data distributions using measures of center and variability. Example 1: The mean height of players on the basketball team is 10 cm greater than the mean height of players on the soccer team. This difference is equal to approximately twice the variability (mean absolute deviation) on either team. On a line plot, note the difference between the two distributions of heights. Example 2: Decide whether the words in a chapter of a seventh-grade science book are generally longer than the words in a chapter of a fourth grade science book.
description_plain: Your child can compare two sets of numbers by looking at their averages and how spread out the numbers are.

subject: Math
description: Use statistical measures to compare two numerical data distributions.
description_plain: Your child uses math tools to compare two groups of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Draw comparative inferences about populations.
description_plain: Your child makes comparisons and conclusions about groups of data.

subject: Math
description: Use data from a random sample to draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic of interest. Example 1: Estimate the mean word length in a book by randomly sampling words from the book. Example 2: Predict the winner of a school election based on randomly sampled survey data.
description_plain: Your child uses information from a random sample to make guesses about a larger group, like estimating average word length in a book.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a sample is a random sample given a real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a sample was chosen randomly in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Use random samples.
description_plain: Use random samples.

subject: Math
description: Use random sampling to draw inferences about a population.
description_plain: Your child uses random samples to learn about bigger groups.

subject: Math
description: Statistics and Probability
description_plain: Statistics and Probability

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, volume, and surface area of twoand three-dimensional objects composed of triangles, quadrilaterals, polygons, cubes, and right prisms. Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child solves problems about area, volume, and surface area of shapes like triangles, rectangles, cubes, and prisms using given formulas.

subject: Math
description: Find the area and circumference of a circle. Solve problems involving area and circumference of a circle(s). Formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child finds the area and distance around circles and solves related problems using formulas.

subject: Math
description: Determine circumference, area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child calculates circumference, area, surface area, and volume of different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify and use properties of angles formed when two parallel lines are cut by a transversal (e.g., angles may include alternate interior, alternate exterior, vertical, corresponding).
description_plain: Your child learns about special angles formed when two parallel lines are crossed by another line and uses their properties.

subject: Math
description: Identify and use properties of supplementary, complementary, and adjacent angles in a multistep problem to write and solve simple equations for an unknown angle in a figure.
description_plain: Your child uses properties of angles that add up to 90 or 180 degrees and solves problems to find unknown angles.

subject: Math
description: Identify, use, and describe properties of angles and their measures.
description_plain: Your child identifies and works with different types of angles and their measures.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving angle measure, circumference, area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child solves real-life and math problems involving angles, circles, and 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Describe the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing three-dimensional figures. Example: Describe plane sections of right rectangular prisms and right rectangular pyramids.
description_plain: Your child describes the flat shapes made when slicing 3D shapes like boxes and pyramids.

subject: Math
description: Use and apply the triangle inequality theorem.
description_plain: Your child uses the triangle inequality rule to solve problems about triangle side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Identify or describe the properties of all types of triangles based on angle and side measures.
description_plain: Your child identifies different types of triangles by their sides and angles.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures, including finding length and area.
description_plain: Your child solves problems using scale drawings to find lengths and areas of shapes.

subject: Math
description: Describe and apply properties of geometric figures.
description_plain: Your child describes and uses properties of different geometric shapes.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding of geometric figures and their properties
description_plain: Your child understands shapes and their special features.

subject: Math
description: Geometry
description_plain: Geometry

subject: Math
description: Determine the reasonableness of answer(s) or interpret the solution(s) in the context of the problem. Example: If you want to place a towel bar that is 9 3/4 inches long in the center of a door that is 27 1/2 inches wide, you will need to place the bar about 9 inches from each edge; this estimate can be used as a check on the exact computation.
description_plain: Your child checks if answers to problems make sense by estimating and comparing to the problem situation.

subject: Math
description: Determine the reasonableness of the answer(s) in problem-solving situations.
description_plain: Your child decides if answers to problems are reasonable.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems leading to inequalities of the form px + q > r or px + q < r, where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers, and graph the solution set of the inequality. Example: A salesperson is paid $50 per week plus $3 per sale. This week she wants her pay to be at least $100. Write an inequality for the number of sales the salesperson needs to make and describe the solutions.
description_plain: Your child solves word problems that use inequalities and graphs the solutions, like figuring out how many sales are needed to earn a certain amount.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems leading to equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r, where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers. Example: The perimeter of a rectangle is 54 cm. Its length is 6 cm. What is its width?
description_plain: Your child solves word problems using equations to find unknown numbers, like finding the width of a rectangle.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent quantities in a real-world or mathematical problem and construct simple equations and inequalities to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child uses letters to stand for numbers in problems and writes simple equations or inequalities to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to calculate with numbers in any form; convert between forms as appropriate. Example: If a woman making $25 an hour gets a 10% raise, she will make an additional 1/10 of her salary an hour, or $2.50, for a new salary of $27.50 an hour (or 1.1 × $25 = $27.50).
description_plain: Your child uses math rules to calculate with numbers and changes between forms, like finding a raise from a percentage.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step real-world and mathematical problems posed with positive and negative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child solves multi-step problems using positive and negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems using numerical and algebraic expressions, equations, and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child solves real-life and math problems using numbers, expressions, equations, and inequalities.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions with rational coefficients. Example 1: The expression 1/2 • (x + 6) is equivalent to 1/2 • x + 3. Example 2: The expression 5.3 – y + 4.2 is equivalent to 9.5 – y (or – y + 9.5). Example 3: The expression 4w – 10 is equivalent to 2(2w – 5).
description_plain: Your child uses math rules to add, subtract, factor, and expand expressions with numbers and letters.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child uses math rules to create expressions that mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Represent expressions in equivalent forms.
description_plain: Your child writes expressions in different but equal forms.

subject: Math
description: Expressions and Equations
description_plain: Expressions and Equations

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve multi-step ratio and percent problems. Examples: simple interest, tax, markups and markdowns, gratuities and commissions, fees, percent increase and decrease.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios and percentages to solve multi-step problems like calculating interest, tax, discounts, tips, commissions, fees, and percent changes.

subject: Math
description: Explain what a point (x, y) on the graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation, with special attention to the points (0, 0) and (1, r), where r is the unit rate.
description_plain: Your child understands what points on a graph mean in a proportional relationship, especially the points (0,0) and (1, unit rate).

subject: Math
description: Represent proportional relationships by equations. Example: If total cost t is proportional to the number n of items purchased at a constant price p, the relationship between the total cost and the number of items can be expressed as t = pn.
description_plain: Your child can write equations to show proportional relationships, like how total cost equals price times number of items.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate (constant of proportionality) from tables, graphs, equations, pictures, or words.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether two quantities are proportionally related (e.g., by testing for equivalent ratios in a table, graphing on a coordinate plane and observing whether the graph is a straight line through the origin).
description_plain: Your child can tell if two amounts are proportional by checking if their ratios are equal or if their graph is a straight line through zero.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of fractions, including ratios of lengths, areas, and other quantities measured in like or different units. Example: If a person walks 1/2 mile in each 1/4 hour, compute the unit rate as the complex fraction 1/2 / 1/4 miles per hour, equivalently 2 miles per hour.
description_plain: Your child can calculate unit rates when given ratios with fractions, like finding miles per hour when given distance and time as fractions.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, recognize, and represent proportional relationships and use them to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use proportional relationships to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate an understanding of proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Ratios and Proportional Relationships
description_plain: Your child learns about ratios and proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to multiply and divide rational numbers, including real-world contexts; demonstrate that the decimal form of a rational number terminates or eventually repeats.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide rational numbers in real-life situations and knows that decimals from these numbers either end or repeat.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction on a horizontal or vertical number line.
description_plain: Your child can show adding and subtracting numbers on a number line going left-right or up-down.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to add and subtract rational numbers, including real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract rational numbers and use these skills to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving the four operations with rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations to add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers and build on what they already know about these operations.

subject: Math
description: The Number System

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real-world and mathematical problems. (Angles must be adjacent and non-overlapping.)
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by adding or subtracting to find missing angles next to each other that don’t overlap.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. With the aid of a protractor, sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles in whole degrees using a protractor and draw angles with a given size.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools and units to sketch an angle and determine angle measurements.
description_plain: Your child can use tools to draw angles and measure how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle; measure and create angles.
description_plain: Your child learns about angles, how to measure them, and how to make them.

subject: Math
description: Translate information from one type of display to another (table, chart, bar graph, or pictograph).
description_plain: Your child can change information from one form like a table to another like a chart, bar graph, or pictograph.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots (line plots must be labeled with common denominators, such as 1/4, 2/4, 3/4).
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with fractions using data shown in line plots.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (e.g., intervals of 1/2, 1/4, or 1/8).
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions like halves, quarters, or eighths.

subject: Math
description: Organize, display, and answer questions based on data.
description_plain: Your child can organize data, show it clearly, and answer questions about it.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Identify time (analog or digital) as the amount of minutes before or after the hour. Example 1: 2:50 is the same as 10 minutes before3:00.Example 2: Quarter past six is the same as 6:15.
description_plain: Your child can tell time by understanding minutes before or after the hour, like knowing 2:50 is 10 minutes before 3:00.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems (may include finding a missing side length). Whole numbers only. The formulas will be provided.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find area and perimeter of rectangles and solve problems, including finding missing side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time (such as elapsed time), liquid volumes, masses of objects; money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals; and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with measurements like distance, time, volume, weight, and money.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including standard units (in., ft, yd, mi; oz., lb; and c, pt, qt, gal), metric units (cm, m, km; g, kg; and mL, L), and time (sec, min, hr, day, wk, mo, and yr). Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. A table of equivalencies will be provided.Example 1: Know that 1 kg is 1,000 times as heavy as 1 g.Example 2: Express the length of a 4-foot snake as 48 in.
description_plain: Your child knows how different units of measurement relate within one system, like how many inches are in a foot, and can change bigger units into smaller ones.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving length, weight (mass), liquid volume, time, area, and perimeter.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems involving length, weight, volume, time, area, and perimeter.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using measurement and change measurements from bigger units to smaller units.

subject: Math
description: Measurement and Data

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two- dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into mirroring parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry (up to two lines of symmetry).
description_plain: Your child can find and draw lines of symmetry on shapes where the shape can be folded to match exactly on both sides.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by whether they have parallel or perpendicular lines or certain angle sizes, and can recognize right triangles.

subject: Math

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can list properties, sort, draw, and recognize flat shapes.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself. Example 1: Given the rule “add 3” and the starting number 1, generate terms in the resulting sequence and observe that the terms alternate between odd and even numbers.Example 2: Given the rule “increase the number of sides by 1” and starting with a triangle, observe that the tops of the shapes alternate between a side and a vertex.
description_plain: Your child can create number or shape patterns that follow a rule and notice extra features in the pattern.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the interval 1 through 100. Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors. Determine whether a given whole number in the interval 1 through 100 is a multiple of a given one- digit number. Determine whether a given whole number in the interval 1 through 100 is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find all factor pairs of numbers from 1 to 100, know that numbers are multiples of their factors, and tell if numbers are prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply number theory concepts to represent numbers in various ways.

subject: Math
description: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Identify the missing symbol (+, –, ×, ÷, =, <, and >) that makes a number sentence true (single-digit divisor only).
description_plain: Your child can find the missing math symbol (+, –, ×, ÷, =, <, or >) to make a number sentence true using single-digit divisors.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step word problems posed with whole numbers using the four operations. Answers will be either whole numbers or have remainders that must be interpreted yielding a final answer that is a whole number. Represent these problems using equations with a symbol or letter standing for the unknown quantity.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems with several steps using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, and write equations with a letter or symbol for the unknown.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison. Example: Know that 3 × 4 can be used to represent that Student A has 4 objects and Student B has 3 times as many objects not just 3 more objects.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that compare amounts by multiplying or dividing, understanding when to multiply instead of just adding.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations. Example 1: Interpret 35 = 5 × 7 as a statement that35 is 5 times as many as 7 and 7 times as many as 5.Example 2: Know that the statement 24 is 3 times as many as 8 can be represented by the equation24 = 3 × 8 or 24 = 8 × 3.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as a way to compare numbers and can write multiplication equations from words that describe these comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Use numbers and symbols to model the concepts of expressions and equations.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.

subject: Math

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can compare two decimal numbers to the hundredths place using >, =, or < and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100. Example: Rewrite 0.62 as 62/100 and vice versa.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators of 10 or 100 as decimals and change decimals back into fractions.

subject: Math
description: Add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100. Example: Express 3/10 as 30/100, and add3/10 + 4/100 = 30/100 + 4/100 = 34/100.
description_plain: Your child can add two fractions with denominators 10 and 100 by changing them to have the same denominator first.

subject: Math
description: Use operations to solve problems involving decimals, including converting between fractions and decimals (may include word problems).

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number by a non-unit fraction (denominators limited to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100 and final answers do not need to be simplified or written as a mixed number). Example: 3 × (5/6) = 15/6
description_plain: Your child can multiply a whole number by a fraction that is not one over a number, without needing to simplify the answer.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number by a unit fraction (denominators limited to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100 and final answers do not need to be simplified or written as a mixed number). Example: 5 × (1/4) = 5/4
description_plain: Your child can multiply a whole number by a fraction with numerator 1, without needing to simplify the answer.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with a common denominator (denominators limited to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100; no regrouping with subtraction; fractions do not need to be simplified; and no improper fractions as the final answers).

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can break apart fractions or mixed numbers into smaller fractions with the same denominator and explain how.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with a common denominator (denominators limited to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100; answers do not need to be simplified; and no improper fractions as the final answer).

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates, and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how differences in animals of the same kind help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects. Use relative frequencies calculated for rows or columns to describe possible associations between the two variables. Example: Given data on whether students have a curfew on school nights and whether they have assigned chores at home, is there evidence that those who have a curfew also tend to have chores?
description_plain: Your child can make and read a two-way table to see if two categories, like curfews and chores, are related.

subject: Math
description: Understand that patterns of association can be seen in bivariate categorical data by displaying frequencies and relative frequencies in a two-way table.
description_plain: Your child understands how to find patterns between two categories by showing counts and percentages in a two-way table.

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and intercept. Example: In a linear model for a biology experiment, interpret a slope of 1.5 cm/hr as meaning that an additional hour of sunlight each day is associated with an additional 1.5 cm in mature plant height.
description_plain: Your child uses a line equation to solve problems and explain what the slope and intercept mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, identify a line of best fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative correlation, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child makes and reads scatter plots to find patterns like clusters, outliers, and whether two things increase or decrease together.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and interpret bivariate data displayed in multiple representations.
description_plain: Your child looks at data with two variables shown in different ways to find and explain patterns.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.
description_plain: Your child explores how two variables relate to each other by looking for patterns.

subject: Math
description: Apply the converse of the Pythagorean theorem to show a triangle is a right triangle.

description_plain: Your child understands and uses the Pythagorean theorem.

subject: Math
description: Given two congruent figures, describe a sequence of transformations that exhibits the congruence between them.
